---
title: "SME Financial Dashboard"
description: "Monthly revenue, costs and profit visualisation built with Python and Plotly."
categories: [Python, Data Visualisation, Plotly]
date: 2025-01-01
code-fold: true
code-tools: true
highlight-style: github
---

## Overview

This dashboard visualises the monthly financial performance of an SME, 
tracking revenue, costs and profit across the year using Python and Plotly.

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

months  = ["Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun",
           "Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"]
revenue = [42, 58, 55, 70, 65, 80, 78, 90, 85, 95, 102, 120]
costs   = [30, 35, 33, 40, 38, 45, 44, 50, 47, 52,  55,  60]
profit  = [r - c for r, c in zip(revenue, costs)]
```

## Revenue vs Costs

```{python}
#| label: revenue-costs
#| fig-cap: "Monthly revenue and costs (€k)"
#| fig-height: 4

fig1 = go.Figure()
fig1.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=months, y=revenue, mode="lines+markers",
    name="Revenue", line=dict(color="#1B2A4A", width=3)))
fig1.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=months, y=costs, mode="lines+markers",
    name="Costs", line=dict(color="#C9A84C", width=3)))
fig1.update_layout(
    plot_bgcolor="#F4F6FA", paper_bgcolor="#F4F6FA",
    yaxis_title="€ (thousands)", legend=dict(orientation="h"))
fig1.show()
```

## Monthly Profit

```{python}
#| label: profit
#| fig-cap: "Monthly profit (€k) — negative months shown in red"
#| fig-height: 4

bar_colors = ["#1B2A4A" if p >= 0 else "#cc3333" for p in profit]
fig2 = go.Figure(go.Bar(x=months, y=profit, marker_color=bar_colors))
fig2.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color="#C9A84C")
fig2.update_layout(
    plot_bgcolor="#F4F6FA", paper_bgcolor="#F4F6FA",
    yaxis_title="€ (thousands)")
fig2.show()
```
